why: The sovereignty block is REQUIRED and closed: exactly the five sub-fields, no more, no fewer (schemas/envelope.schema.json properties.sovereignty). Baseline accept.
model_class is in the block's required list; an envelope missing any of the five sub-fields fails validation (src/envelope.ts:156-178).
additionalProperties:false on the block — an unknown sub-field is rejected, not ignored (schema line 74).
data_residency pattern is ^[A-Z]{2}$ — uppercase only (schema line 51-54).

INVERTED by grill D5 (fail-closed, closes OD-4): the residency value registry is CLOSED (ISO 3166-1 assigned + EU); an unassigned code (ZZ) is rejected at envelope validation. This is what closes the §5.4 evasion vector. The deployed shape-only validator accepts it — a named conformance defect until the registry gate ships (myelin#11).

INVERTED by grill D2 (closes OD-1): frontier_ok:false + model_class:'frontier' is semantically unsatisfiable (no cloud model may process it, only frontier models permitted) and MUST be rejected at envelope validation — a contradiction is a malformed declaration, not a runtime routing surprise. The deployed schema accepts it (independent fields) — named conformance defect.

parseSovereignty (src/envelope.ts:614-629) is myelin's ONLY reader of frontier_ok/model_class: canReachFrontier = frontier_ok && model_class!=='local-only'. It is advisory — no myelin enforcement path calls it (OD-1).
Baseline: a local envelope to an in-allowlist local.* subject passes the classification budget and allowed_subjects (engine.ts + egress.ts).
CLASSIFICATION_PREFIX_BUDGET: local may target only local.* — a local envelope cannot publish to a federated.* subject (egress.ts). Holds even with block_local_escape off.
block_local_escape fast-path (engine.ts:99-106): a local envelope whose target does not begin with local. is blocked before rule evaluation; the reason string carries 'block_local_escape'.

INVERTED by grill D4 (closes OD-3): prefix<->classification is STRICT EQUALITY per ratified RFC-0002 §8.3 (binding vectors prefix/aligns-local, prefix/mismatch-rejected). A public-classified envelope on a local.* subject is a protocol violation; the deployed CLASSIFICATION_PREFIX_BUDGET downward-superset allows it — named conformance defect. The internal-copy use case re-publishes a distinct local-classified envelope.

MASKING: residency gating works when the code IS a key in data_residency_constraints and the subject matches — this correct path masks the fail-open in the paired vector egress/residency-unlisted-fail-open.
A CH-constrained envelope targeting a federated.de.* subject not matching the CH pattern list is blocked (checkDataResidency, egress.ts).

RE-SCOPED by grill D5: given a REGISTRY-VALID code, a rule with no constraints entry for it imposes no residency constraint — deliberate policy semantics, not fail-open (other gates still apply). The evasion vector (invalid code) is closed upstream at validation (residency/unassigned-code-rejected). Conformant ONLY with the upstream registry gate present.

Fail-closed on unsigned: no signed_by identity => unknown-principal, INDEPENDENT of reject_unknown_partners (ingress.ts). An unsigned envelope can never satisfy ingress. [D0] LINK question ('who delivered this crossing') — keys on the last stamp s[n-1]; with no signed_by there is no s[n-1] to identify the delivering principal, so it fails closed. The AUTHORITY question (actor scope, capability ceiling incl. the §6.2 cap) anchors on the origin s[0] per RFC-0004 §5.5 and is not exercised here.

Baseline federated crossing: last-stamp identity in imported_principals, subject inside local_scope, requirements subset of max_capabilities (ingress.ts checkScopeCeiling). [D0] LINK question: the last-stamp identity s[n-1] is the delivering principal, matched against imported_principals; the recognized partner's scope ceiling (local_scope/max_capabilities) then applies. The AUTHORITY question (actor scope, §6.2 cap) anchors on s[0] per RFC-0004 §5.5 — a separate question, not exercised by this vector.

A mapped partner whose source subject falls outside its local_scope is scope-exceeded (checkScopeCeiling, ingress.ts). [D0] LINK question: the ceiling checked here is the recognized partner's local_scope, keyed via the last-stamp s[n-1] mapping — the delivering-partner contract, not the actor's AUTHORITY. The AUTHORITY question (actor scope, §6.2 cap) anchors on the origin s[0] per RFC-0004 §5.5.

A requirements[] tag outside max_capabilities is scope-exceeded — the capability ceiling constrains a MAPPED partner (ingress.ts). [D0] LINK question: this is the PARTNER mapping's capability ceiling (what the recognized delivering partner may carry), keyed via the last-stamp s[n-1] mapping — distinct from the ACTOR-authority ceiling (§6.2 cap), which anchors on the origin s[0] per RFC-0004 §5.5.

Grill D6 (closes OD-5): permissive mode still ALLOWS an unmapped principal — but under the DEFAULT scope/ceiling, no longer unconditionally. This vector's subject is inside the default scope. The deployed unconditional-allow (bypasses §6.3 entirely) is a named conformance defect; see ingress/unknown-principal-permissive-ceiling-block for the constrained half. [D0] LINK question: the last-stamp principal s[n-1] is unmapped; permissive mode admits it under the default ceiling. The AUTHORITY question (actor scope, §6.2 cap) anchors on the origin s[0] per RFC-0004 §5.5.

With reject_unknown_partners=true an unmapped principal is unknown-principal — the fail-closed posture the operator guide's minimum policy ships (sovereignty-operator.md 2). [D0] LINK question: keyed on the last stamp s[n-1] — an unmapped delivering principal is refused under the strict posture. The AUTHORITY question (actor scope, §6.2 cap) anchors on the origin s[0] per RFC-0004 §5.5.

FINDING: the F-5 enforcement channel's own nak default source (transport.ts:38) has 2 segments, but the envelope source grammar (RFC-0003) requires exactly 3 — {principal}.{stack}.{assistant}. The nak ships via the raw transport (bypassing validateEnvelope), so any subscriber that schema-validates inbound rejects the very message explaining the block (OD-6).

THE TRUST-INVERSION CLOSER (grill D6, closes OD-5): in permissive mode an UNMAPPED principal is constrained by the default scope/ceiling — a subject/capability outside it is blocked exactly as it would be for a declared partner. Declaring a partner MUST NOT reduce its access relative to a stranger's. FAILS against the deployed unconditional-allow — that is the point. [D0] LINK question: keyed on lastStampPrincipal (the last stamp s[n-1]); the default scope/ceiling then bounds the unmapped delivering principal. The AUTHORITY question (actor scope, §6.2 cap) anchors on the origin s[0] per RFC-0004 §5.5.

T-6.1 chain-of-stamps sovereignty (RFC-0005 §6.1; engine.ts validateIngress runs verifyChainSovereignty FIRST, validators/chain.ts). With verify_delegation_sovereignty=true and reject_unknown_partners=true, a multi-stamp (≥2) delegation chain is walked and each stamp identity must have a scope mapping; the earliest unmapped hop invalidates the whole delegation (first-fail-wins) with compliance_block:chain-invalid, independent of the last-stamp check. scope_mappings is empty, so stamp 0 (did:mf:agent.acme.ops.delegator) is unmapped and the chain is rejected. Exercises the registered chain-invalid NakReasonCode, previously covered by no vector (myelin#236 item 21). RFC-0005 §6.1 defers the full chain-walk TREATMENT; this pins the registered enum value against the deployed engine behaviour. [D0] LINK question, chain-walk variant: with verify_delegation_sovereignty on, each hop identity (s[0..n-1]) must be a mapped delivering principal — still the LINK question (who delivered/relayed), not AUTHORITY. The full chain-walk treatment is deferred; the AUTHORITY question (actor scope, §6.2 cap) anchors on the origin s[0] per RFC-0004 §5.5.

LINK-LEVEL rejection (RFC-0005 §6.0). The crossing arrived from partner network 'stranger' (sourceSubject federated.stranger.>), which is NOT in the local partner registry — the only configured partner_network is 'acme' (local_scope federated.acme.>). Under reject_unknown_partners=true an unregistered partner is refused with compliance_block:partner-unknown BEFORE the last-stamp principal is evaluated (§6.1). Distinct from unknown-principal (identity-level: a recognized partner carrying a principal outside imported_principals). Exercises the registered partner-unknown NakReasonCode, previously covered by no vector (semantics decided on #240; myelin#236 item 21). The deployed engine collapses this into unknown-principal — a named conformance defect (myelin#11); the rule is the contract. [D0] The LINK question in its purest form: the §6.0 partner check gates which federation link may deliver at all — evaluated before the last-stamp principal (s[n-1]) is even read. The AUTHORITY question (actor scope, §6.2 cap, origin s[0]) is separate (RFC-0004 §5.5).

Grill D3 (closes OD-2): max_hop is an origin-declared forwarding TTL — len(chain)-1 <= max_hop. max_hop:0 = origin-only ACCEPTS the directly-signed 1-stamp envelope (0 forwards). The deployed cortex gate (len(chain) > max_hop) rejects this — the named off-by-one defect.
max_hop:0 rejects any forwarded copy (1 forward > 0). The TTL is enforced against the RFC-0004 signature chain — no mutable counter, no decrement, signatures stay valid.
3 stamps = origin + 2 forwards = exactly max_hop:2 — allowed at the boundary.

Grill D9 (closes OD-8): imported_principals entries MUST be principal-class DIDs; an agent-class entry is rejected at configuration validation. Trust in the ingress mapping is per-principal (ADR-0013, RFC-0006 roster); the matcher compares the principal component extracted from the last stamp's agent-class identity. [D0] Config guard for the LINK-question roster: imported_principals is the set of delivering principals matched against the last stamp s[n-1]. No crossing anchor is exercised (this validates config shape); the AUTHORITY question (actor scope, §6.2 cap) anchors on the origin s[0] per RFC-0004 §5.5.
